info.title: Tessera API
info.description: Customer-facing REST API for Tessera's gold Hyperliquid datasets. Browse the catalog, list partitions, and mint presigned download URLs. Download this document at `/v1/openapi.json` to generate a client.

servers.description: Same-origin (production fronts the API via CloudFront)

get.description:
Returns every gold dataset visible to the caller's policy, with a partition
count, the distinct coins, and the month range available.

Returns the partitions (coin × month) of `asset` visible to the caller,
optionally narrowed by the `coin` and `month` query filters.

parameters.name: coin
parameters.description: Only return partitions for this coin, e.g. `BTC`.

parameters.name: month
parameters.description: Only return partitions for this month (`YYYY-MM`).

403.description: Dataset not permitted by the caller's policy

get.description:
Mints a short-lived presigned URL for the parquet object of a single
`(asset, coin, month)` partition. By default this **302-redirects** to that
URL, so `curl -L`/`wget` download the file in one step. Send
`Accept: application/json` to get the URL (and its expiry) as a JSON body
instead — useful for programmatic callers.

200.description: Presigned download URL as JSON. Returned only when the request sends `Accept: application/json`.

302.description: Redirect (Location header) to the presigned parquet URL. The default — follow it with `curl -L`.

403.description: Dataset not permitted by the caller's policy

404.description: Partition not found or hidden by policy

partition_count.description: Number of partitions visible to the caller.

generated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp the catalog manifest was generated.

expires_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp at which the URL expires.

url.description: Presigned Tigris URL for the parquet object.

ErrorBody.description:
The error response body. Every error variant serialises to this shape —
a single machine-readable `error` code. Declared as a real struct (rather
than the inline `json!` below) only so it can be referenced as a response
`body` in the OpenAPI spec; `into_response` still emits the same JSON.

is_open.description:
Whether this is the current, in-progress month — its parquet is
re-materialised daily (part-month freshness) and therefore grows under
the customer. Absent (`None`) in older (v1) manifests.

modified_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last write, if known.

asset.description: Dataset name the partitions belong to.

generated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp the catalog manifest was generated.

api_key.description: Raw API key sent as a bearer token.

tags.name: datasets
tags.description: Browse and download gold datasets (authenticated with an API key).
